metadata.title: Deploy a Pattern-Analysis Subagent for a Grading Tool with Claude Code

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: I teach, and I want a subagent that scans a batch of student submissions for recurring patterns without dragging every essay into my main session. I'm building a grading tool in Claude Code and the pattern analysis is the piece I'm unsure how to isolate. Help me: (1) write the subagent's system prompt so it reads the submissions in its own context and returns only patterns; (2) define the three-section summary format it should hand back; (3) specify which tools it's allowed so it can read but never edit my grade file.

props.command: I teach, and I want a subagent that scans a batch of student submissions for recurring patterns without dragging every essay into my main session. I'm building a grading tool in Claude Code and the pattern analysis is the piece I'm unsure how to isolate. Help me: (1) write the subagent's system prompt so it reads the submissions in its own context and returns only patterns; (2) define the three-section summary format it should hand back; (3) specify which tools it's allowed so it can read but never edit my grade file.
props.segment: Deploy a Pattern-Analysis Subagent for a

beats.narration: Policy research fills 48% of the session window. A subagent does the same research and returns three sentences, leaving the main build clean.

A subagent is the discipline of keeping the main build clean by delegating context-heavy work. The summary that comes back is all the main session needs — the policy doc and submission files stay isolated.
Ask Claude to write the subagent definition with YAML frontmatter specifying a read-only tool set.

props.command:
claude "Write subagent definition:
  .claude/agents/pattern-analyzer.md
  inputs: submissions dir, rubric file
  tools: Read, Grep, Glob ONLY
  output: common_misconceptions (list)
           severity_by_section (dict)
           recommended_focus (3-sentence max)
  YAML frontmatter: name, description, tools"

beats.narration: The subagent definition: YAML frontmatter with name, description, and a Read-Grep-Glob tool whitelist. The workflow scans submissions against rubric criteria and outputs structured data — no grades, no Write calls.

props.code:
---
name: pattern-analyzer
description: Scans student submissions for
  rubric-criteria misconception patterns.
  Returns structured report without grades.
tools: [Read, Grep, Glob]
---

# Pattern Analyzer

Accept: submissions_dir (str), rubric_file (str)

1. Read rubric_file → extract criteria names
2. For each submission in submissions_dir:
   Grep for each criterion → flag present/absent
3. Aggregate: count missing per criterion
4. Output:
   common_misconceptions: [criterion list]
   severity_by_section: {section: count}
   recommended_focus: <3-sentence max>

beats.narration: Subagent invoked on three submissions. It runs in isolation — Read, Grep, Glob only, no Write or Edit. The main session context meter stays flat while the subagent processes all three files. It returns a structured report: two common misconceptions, severity by section, one recommended focus sentence.
Add a WriterReviewer second subagent that reviews the pattern-analyzer output from a fresh context window.

props.command:
claude "Add WriterReviewer pattern:
  second subagent reviews pattern-analyzer output
  from a fresh context window.
  Catches errors the first subagent missed."

beats.narration: The reviewer subagent catches one error: the first subagent flagged loop termination as missing across all three submissions, but submission two addressed it in the conclusion section rather than the body. Fresh context, different reading. The WriterReviewer pattern improves accuracy without touching the main session.

A subagent definition is three things: a tool whitelist, a structured output format, and an isolation contract. The main session delegates the context-heavy work, receives the structured summary, and stays clean for the build.
Next: build a simulation with the three-file system.
Like and subscribe for more Claude Code for Teachers.
